pi_items.source: String literal checked in Firefox bookmarks database by export_result.sh line 63
Task description requires saving to ~/Documents/fridge_selection.json; matches metadata path
Task description explicitly states: 'Filter for Total Volume: Between 16.0 and 19.0 cubic feet'
Task description explicitly states: 'Filter for Total Volume: Between 16.0 and 19.0 cubic feet'
ENERGY STAR formula for top freezer refrigerators: 210.3 + 7.26 × cubic_feet. For 16 cu ft: 210.3 + (7.26 × 16) = 326.46 kWh/yr. Confirmed by multiple sources including Department of Energy guidance.

ENERGY STAR formula for top freezer refrigerators: 210.3 + 7.26 × cubic_feet. For 19 cu ft: 210.3 + (7.26 × 19) = 348.24 kWh/yr. The 450 value provides additional margin but realistic max for 16-19 cu ft top freezers is ~348 kWh/yr.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the real ENERGY STAR Product Finder for certified residential refrigerators (energystar.gov). The volume range of 16.0-19.0 cubic feet is verified from task description. However, the plausible energy consumption range in metadata (200-450 kWh/yr) contradicts the actual ENERGY STAR standard: top freezer refrigerators in the 16-19 cubic feet range have maximum allowed annual energy consumption calculated as 210.3 + (7.26 × capacity) kWh/yr, yielding a realistic range of approximately 326-348 kWh/yr. The minimum of 200 kWh is implausibly low; the maximum of 450 kWh provides excessive margin above realistic values.
